summary: Verified date/time input height stability fix on mobile (390x844) and desktop (1280x900). Filling values produces ZERO height change (0.00px diff). All CSS assertions pass.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Height lock fix (height:46px + line-height:1.25 on .date-native + ::-webkit-date-and-time-value + ::-webkit-datetime-edit constraints) is confirmed working on chromium at both mobile 390x844 and desktop 1280x900 viewports. Height is stable (0px diff) between empty and filled states for both date and time inputs. Note: iOS Safari WebKit-specific quirk cannot be reproduced in chromium test browser, but the CSS rules that neutralize it are verified present and applied (computed height=46px, lineHeight=20px in both states).
